title: [rootless] podman ps doesn't show anything, even though container is running
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman ps has no output even though container is running. I am running rootless and starting the containers using a systemd service. I am also unable to run podman exec on any of the running containers because I get the following error 
**Error: can only create exec sessions on running containers: container state improper"**.

Running podman ps --all shows the containers. Running podman ps --all --sync does not resolve the problem. 

```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED     STATUS   PORTS               NAMES
08d91c459257  localhost/gitlab-runner:latest  run --user=gitlab...  7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  gitlab-runner
15dbaf24744f  localhost/satis:latest          satis-server          7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  satis
3eb12fe80b76  localhost/proxy:latest          /usr/sbin/nginx       7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  proxy
81b11ec6843f  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                                  7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  77502afe878b-infra
e1cabe9de0ec  localhost/gitlab:latest         /assets/wrapper       7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  gitlab
```

I assume the issue links somehow to the path **/tmp/run-1000/containers** being used as discussed in this ticket (was told to open another issue) #1680

/tmp being tidied after 7 days perhaps? 

Unit file is below

```
[Unit]
Description=Devops service
Wants=network.target
After=network-online.target

[Service]
User=repo.manager
Group=repo.manager
TimeoutStartSec=10m
Restart=on-failure
KillMode=none
Type=forking
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
ExecStart=/usr/local/bin/devops-start
ExecStop=/usr/local/bin/devops-stop

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
```

/usr/local/bin/devops-start contains a series of podman build and start commands

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Create a systemd service that runs some containers on boot as a rootless user

2. Log in as the rootless user - run podman ps. Initially everything will look fine. 

3. Run same command a week later and podman ps output will be empty. 

**Describe the results you received:**
podman ps is empty

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                           COMMAND               CREATED     STATUS   PORTS               NAMES
08d91c459257  localhost/gitlab-runner:latest  run --user=gitlab...  7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  gitlab-runner
15dbaf24744f  localhost/satis:latest          satis-server          7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  satis
3eb12fe80b76  localhost/proxy:latest          /usr/sbin/nginx       7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  proxy
81b11ec6843f  k8s.gcr.io/pause:3.2                                  7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  77502afe878b-infra
e1cabe9de0ec  localhost/gitlab:latest         /assets/wrapper       7 days ago  Created  0.0.0.0:80->80/tcp  gitlab
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Only happens after a week or so. I can monitor daily to double check when exactly it occurs but the key thing is you have to leave the services running as rootless for X amount of time for the issue to occur. I assume its got something to do with the /tmp directory being cleaned up by RHEL8. 

**Output of `podman version`:**
```
Version:      2.0.5
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.7
Built:        Wed Sep 23 17:18:02 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No - sticking with RHEL8 official binaries for now...

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
```

comments.body: If you're certain the container processes are actually running, then this is definitely `/tmp` being cleaned. The tmpfiles.d script at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/contrib/tmpfile/podman.conf should prevent this from happening - we'll be shipping it in the package itself as of RHEL 8.4, but you can work around it for now by manually installing said config file to `/etc/tmpfiles.d/`

Thanks, I will give that a try. 

However before I do I noticed that the file you reference has the following paths...

`
x /tmp/podman-run-*
x /tmp/containers-user-*
D! /run/podman 0700 root root
D! /var/lib/cni/networks
`

But my runroot path is /tmp/run-1000/containers which doesn’t appear to match any of the rules in the file? Will that simply not work?

Would expect something like the below in the file? 

`
x /tmp/run-*/containers
`

How did you able to run as non root, i am getting this error 
**Error: cannot setup namespace using newuidmap: exit status 1**

@developedsoftware Yes, I would definitely add that. The paths we've used changed at some point in the past (the new ones are much more obvious as to who is using the directory in `/tmp`) and it looks like the old ones are not included in the file.

@SahilChaudhary25 Please open a new issue.

For anybody having the same issue as this...I have added the following conf file to /etc/tmpfiles.d/podman.conf

`x /tmp/run-*`

Have restarted and will report back in 7 days if the fix was successful. 

@mheon can you confirm that looks OK? and that this will not be needed when RHEL 8.4 ships? Should I remove this file when RHEL 8.4 ships?

Yes, that looks fine. You may still need the file after 8.4, though, since we are not including the `/tmp/run-*` paths in it. We can get them in, but that will probably be later than 8.4, which is mostly finalized at this point.

OK thanks for confirming. Will close once I have confirmed this is resolved. 

Is there a simple way of changing the runroot path? I could then just move this out of /tmp and into the users home directory and then the issue would not occur (and the above file would not be needed)...
Can confirm this resolves the issue. Closing.
